behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (HTTP raw-body extraction -> signature verifier -> queue call suppression), not just pure hash logic. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required; in-process app + real middleware wiring is enough, so System is disqualified by behavior text focusing on signature/body mismatch and forwarding suppression only. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real network conditions, only correct in-process wiring and response/forwarding behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Start the HTTP handler in-process with the real raw-body middleware and real signature verification module, but a test queue adapter that records writes. Send a webhook with a deliberately wrong `X-Signature-256` for the exact raw payload bytes. Assert HTTP 401 and assert zero queue writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is explicit cross-module ordering (handler -> persistent queue -> HTTP response), so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local in-process module wiring plus a real persistence-backed queue implementation; no required real external network/subprocess, so System is not required by the behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text requires persistence ordering, not real OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler with the real queue implementation backed by a local test store. Instrument enqueue completion and response completion timestamps/events. Send a valid webhook and assert enqueue durability/commit event occurs before 200 is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears at orchestration boundary between request path and async consumer path. Unit test of a single function can miss wiring mistakes in async handoff. REAL-INFRA: no need for real network/OS/subprocess; in-process modules with a deliberately slow processor catches it. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about internal async decoupling, not real infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Use real handler + queue handoff path in-process, with downstream processor intentionally blocked/sleeping. Send a valid request and assert response returns quickly while processor completion remains pending at response time.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency and HTTP response timing, which can be hidden by in-process fakes and mocked timers. MOCK-HIDE: mocked networking/scheduling can pass while real event-loop contention/backpressure fails. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires real wall-clock SLA under concurrent load conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service as a real process with real HTTP requests against it. Preload slow concurrent downstream work (earlier events), then send new valid webhook requests and measure end-to-end response latency. Assert each response is <= 3 seconds wall-clock.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness is at state handoff/storage boundary (incoming event -> dedupe check -> persistent write). Unit logic alone can miss persistence interaction bugs. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with local persistent test store; no mandatory real network/subprocess in behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text does not require real external infrastructure, only correct persistent dedupe behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler + real persistent queue/dedupe storage in-process. Send the same event ID twice (separate requests). Assert first request is accepted per design and exactly one durable queue record exists for that ID after both requests.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is module coordination (queue-full signal -> HTTP status/header mapping), so Unit may miss wiring/translation issues. REAL-INFRA: no required real OS/network/subprocess; deterministic in-process queue-full condition is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about error mapping from queue state to HTTP response, not real infra timing/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process HTTP handler with the real error-mapping path and a queue implementation/config that deterministically reports capacity full. Send a valid request and assert HTTP 503 plus correct `Retry-After` header, and no enqueue side effect.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates real queue/storage module interaction and persisted capacity accounting, not just a pure function. REAL-INFRA: local persistence (DB/filesystem) integration is sufficient; behavior text does not require real external network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because the behavior is queue capacity enforcement in its own persistence layer, which can be tested with local integration infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with a small configured capacity in a local test store. Enqueue events until capacity is reached, then attempt one more enqueue. Assert the overflow enqueue is rejected and previously accepted events remain durable and unchanged.
